entry.title: MAD-HTLC
entry.summary:
The recent research findings concerning Hash Time-Locked Contracts (HTLC) have brought to light significant insights into their security dependencies. The study reveals that the security of HTLCs largely depends on the altruistic behavior of miners, or at the very least, their short-term outlook. This characteristic might suffice temporarily; however, adjustments were necessary to modify bitcoind to reduce reliance on this assumption. Specifically, it required about 150 lines of code to alter bitcoind to operate in a less myopic fashion.
In addition to addressing the vulnerabilities associated with traditional HTLC systems, the research team has developed an alternative known as MAD-HTLC. This new mechanism is designed to be provably secure, ensuring that it is in every participant's best interest to act according to the system’s expectations. This advancement not only strengthens the reliability of conducting transactions but also aligns with broader security objectives.
For further details on these findings, the technical report and a brief explanatory post can be accessed through the following links: [Technical Report on arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/2006.12031) and [Blog Post by Ittay Eyal](https://ittayeyal.github.io/2020-06-22-mad-htlc). The authors welcome feedback and have engaged with relevant teams to discuss these developments.
